title: Lesson Quiz: Selecting Portfolio Artifacts
description: Assessment of the concept that Choosing evidence of skills and achievements.

choices.text: A piece of evidence that shows skill, achievement, or growth
choices.feedback: Correct. Portfolio artifacts are selected pieces of evidence that help demonstrate what a student can do.

choices.text: Any random item with no purpose
choices.feedback: Artifacts should be selected because they support the purpose of the portfolio.

choices.text: Only a list of future goals
choices.feedback: Goals may support a portfolio, but artifacts are evidence of skills, work, or accomplishments.
Artifacts are stronger when they are selected thoughtfully and connected to what the student wants to show.

questions.prompt: Which artifact would best show communication skills?

choices.feedback: A blank page does not show evidence of communication skill.
Artifacts should connect directly to the skill being shown.
This does not give clear evidence of communication skill.

choices.text: A presentation outline with teacher feedback
choices.feedback: Correct. A presentation outline and feedback can show how a student communicates ideas clearly.

questions.prompt: Strong artifact selection should connect to the student's skills, achievements, or goals.

choices.feedback: Correct. Artifacts should be chosen because they support the student's purpose and show meaningful evidence.
Artifacts should not be random. They should match the student's skills, growth, or goals.

questions.prompt: Why might a student include a reflection with an artifact?

choices.text: To explain why the artifact matters and what it shows
choices.feedback: This is a strong reason, but the correct choice in this set is the more complete statement about explaining skill and growth.

choices.text: To make the artifact less clear
choices.feedback: Reflections should improve clarity, not reduce it.

choices.text: To explain how the artifact shows skill, growth, or achievement
choices.feedback: Correct. A reflection helps the reviewer understand why the artifact was selected and what it demonstrates.
Reflections support strong evidence; they do not replace it.

questions.prompt: A portfolio should include every available artifact, even if some do not support the portfolio purpose.

choices.feedback: A strong portfolio should be selective and include items that support the student's purpose.
Correct. Students should choose artifacts carefully instead of adding everything available.
